behaviors.description: Primary translator produces grammatically coherent translation that preserves meaning
behaviors.justification: Coherence and semantic preservation are properties of actual neural network output, not deterministic computation. A mock translator would always return the same string, completely hiding real failures in API output quality. The test must call the real neural machine translation API to verify that actual model outputs are coherent and meaningful.
behaviors.test_description: Call the service with diverse test inputs (simple sentences, complex sentences, ambiguous phrases, domain-specific text). Receive translations from the real neural API. Verify each output is grammatically correct and preserves the semantic meaning of the source. Use automated checks (LLM-based evaluation or embedding similarity) or manual spot-checks against reference translations.

behaviors.description: When translation API returns 503, service transparently falls back without error to caller
behaviors.justification: The behavior spans multiple components: HTTP client (error detection), fallback router (decision logic), and fallback executor (SQLite lookup). Testing only the error-detection code path (Unit with mocked HTTP response) would miss failures in the actual fallback execution. The service must be tested with real HTTP error responses and a working fallback system to verify the full transparent fallback works end-to-end without returning errors.
behaviors.test_description: Set up a test HTTP mock server or stub configured to return 503 for translation requests. Create a test SQLite database pre-populated with phrase translations. Call the service with a request. Verify: (1) no 503 error is returned to the caller, (2) the fallback dictionary is queried, (3) a translation result is returned successfully.

behaviors.description: Dictionary-based fallback returns correct pre-stored translation for phrases in SQLite
behaviors.justification: The behavior's correctness depends on actual SQLite database I/O and query execution. Mocking the database lookup hides failures in SQL construction, connection handling, or data retrieval. The fallback must be tested against a real (or in-memory test) SQLite database to catch query failures, data inconsistencies, or edge cases in phrase matching.
behaviors.test_description: Create a test SQLite database with known phrase-translation pairs (including edge cases: empty strings, special characters, punctuation, case sensitivity). Insert test data and verify the fallback lookup returns exactly the stored translation for each phrase, and returns null or 'not found' for missing phrases.

behaviors.description: Quality tracking computes character-level edit distance and stores metric
behaviors.justification: While edit distance computation is a pure algorithm (Unit-testable), the behavior requires both computation AND persistence. Mocking storage would hide failures in how the metric is written to the database, file system, or monitoring system. Testing only the algorithm misses storage failures like missing fields, type mismatches, or query errors. The test must verify the metric is actually stored in the real system.
behaviors.test_description: Generate both primary and fallback translations. Verify: (1) edit distance is computed correctly against known test cases, (2) the computed metric is actually stored in the real storage system (query the database or inspect logs to confirm the metric exists with correct field values and data types).

behaviors.description: When both translators available, return primary translation and log quality comparison
behaviors.justification: This behavior orchestrates multiple components (primary API, fallback, comparison, logging) and its correctness depends on the real neural translator API. Mocking the primary translator hides critical failures like: service crashes on specific API response formats, service hangs on slow API, service fails to parse real API output, or service returns fallback instead of primary. The service must be tested with the real neural API to ensure it correctly integrates both translators and handles real API behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Set up both primary translator API (real) and fallback dictionary (SQLite test database) as operational. Call the service with a request. Verify: (1) the real neural translator is called, (2) the result returned to the caller is the primary translation (not the fallback), (3) quality comparison is computed and logged in the background (inspect logs or a metrics system to confirm).
